description:
Support ticket #215473973691221 in tx2p130c (Intercomrades app 6) escalated from CS.

CUSTOMER: ClickSend (workspace nqkkwhx0) + ~60 other affected workspaces.
ERROR: "'{attr}' value '{value}' is not in the list of allowed values"
CONTROLLER: Api::V3::CompaniesController (POST /companies)
IMPACT: ~5k errors/hr in EU, ~40k/hr on companies controller per reporter.
DEPLOY: 2026-04-16 ~15:52 UTC (4 days ago).
SUSPECTED COMMIT (per ticket): a9d0694ab1 - UNVERIFIED, preliminary analysis suggests actual commit is ee1ae1ca85a8 (PR #497043 by Marc Meillac).
URGENCY: Critical per reporter; no incident declared yet.
REPORTER: Radoslav A (CS: API & Integrations Tech [SBR]), back-office ticket.
CURRENT ASSIGNEE: [R&D] Team Integration Capabilities (potentially misrouted - PR author is on Team Conversations).

Investigation to confirm root cause, blast radius, rollback options, and correct R&D ownership before drafting response.

description: Deep dive written at plans/2026-04-22-dc-custom-audience-release-plan.md. Key findings: (1) PR #123355 is SAFE for Fin-usage custom actions — action.fin? auto-enum at action.rb:164 routes :custom to process_custom_ruleset (preserve). (2) Critical invariant: fin-tab.tsx:43 only mounts audience row when usage==='fin'. (3) Recommended approach E: #123355 + 5-line derive-audienceMode + disable dropdown for unrepresentable rulesets + explicit ruleset:null on active flip-to-Everyone. (4) Stretch goal B: merge #123080 with flag OFF. Blocker: Buildkite RED on 55e9ac4.
title: [DC] Custom-audience release-day deep dive 2026-04-22
GH-478664: Dev Hub app review submissions not appearing in admin tools

description:
Closeout plan for 17 open PRs + Slack-driven action items, after DC React UI launched 2026-04-25 to 6 beta customers. Post-launch lens: stabilization & impact, not crunch.

## Source data
- 17 open PRs by anubhav-intercom across intercom + embercom
- Slack threads: D0A9VMKKBQU (Vedran DM, both PRs MERGED), C0AS4KQFX16 (Ellice farewell handoff)
- Channel #data-connectors-wg past 14 days

## Priority order
- T0 Janitorial: close superseded PRs (5 min)
- T1 Drafts → ready: 4 DC polish PRs (#122623/4/5/7)
- T2 Bug fix merges: standalone DC fixes (#122308, #122123, #123054)
- T3 Stripe backend pair (#497686 → #497688)
- T4 Stale PR triage: #121472, #491711, #120152, #120677
- T5 Custom audience deeper PRs: #123409 + #121984 (needs decision)
- T6 Ellice farewell ownership decisions (4 issues, 3 open PRs)
- T7 Design questions tracking (data inputs, used-by behavior, health metric)
title: DC React migration — open-PR closeout (post-launch stabilization)

description:
Both have my own "Closing this" comments but are still OPEN — I never clicked close.

- intercom#491711 — Add fin_prompt column. Commented "Closing — description and Fin prompt are intentionally the same field." Then design flipped 3 weeks later: we ADDED internal_description (#499514, merged) for the OPPOSITE outcome. Definitely obsolete.
- intercom#499714 — Emit GenericAttribute fields explicitly in as_json. Commented "Closing this as scope creep that wasn't in the original request. The frontend fix (intercom/embercom#123243) resolves #494520 independently."

Action: gh pr close 491711 --repo intercom/intercom; gh pr close 499714 --repo intercom/intercom
title: T0: Close 2 superseded PRs (#491711, #499714)

description:
All 4 are 7d old and likely just need a final pass before un-drafting. Each has known scope.

- embercom#122624 — Polish health page (description, URL block, zero-execution success rate). NOT draft. Updated 6d ago = review feedback to address. Closest to merge.
- embercom#122623 (DRAFT) — Invalidate cached test result when request config changes. **EXPANDED SCOPE per Slack:** Anubhav promised Ana to also add 'name not required to save draft' fix here. Verify scope, finish, un-draft.
- embercom#122625 (DRAFT) — Data tab scroll-to-top, stable hover height, root-object filtering parity.
- embercom#122627 (DRAFT) — Fin tab full-width header above preview, standard toggle.

Action: For each, /quality-check + verify scope vs current launch state, push, un-draft, request review.
title: T1: Un-draft + merge 4 DC polish PRs

description:
Independent bug fixes, not draft, just need review/merge push.

- embercom#122308 — Show version note dialog on first publish + dialog spacing. 11d old, updated 10d ago.
- embercom#122123 — Fix DC deletion error and blank screen in MCP actions list. 12d old, updated 7d ago — has activity.
- embercom#123054 — Fix nil description validation for auto-generated data inputs. 6d old.

Action: For each: poke reviewers, address feedback, merge.
title: T2: Merge 3 standalone DC bug fixes

description:
Hard sequential dependency: backfill rake task is useless until type fix is deployed.

- intercom#497686 — Fix Stripe template timestamp field types misclassified as integer. 10d old, updated 5d ago — actively being reviewed. This must merge AND deploy first.
- intercom#497688 — Add backfill rake task. 10d old. Wait for #497686 to be in production, then merge.

Action: Push #497686 to merge → wait for prod deploy → merge #497688 → run backfill rake task.
title: T3: Land Stripe backend pair (#497686 → #497688)

description:
All 4 PRs are 17+ days old and accruing rebase debt. Per-PR decision.

- embercom#121472 (17d) — Client-side pagination on Data tab tree table. Decision: still wanted? If yes, rebase. If superseded by tree table rewrite work, close.
- embercom#120677 (26d) — /dc-migrate skill upgrade with bead tracking, schema parity, multi-viewport. Likely still useful but needs rebase.
- embercom#120152 (28d, very stale) — /dc-qa skill upgrade with 473 test scenarios. Likely superseded by per-PR QA work. Close-as-superseded?
- embercom#122798 (7d, NOT stale) — /migration-plan integration into ember-to-react-migration skill. ACTIVE: I referenced this pipeline in the custom-audience Slack thread. Worth merging soon.

Action: For each, decide rebase vs close, then act. Default to closing if rationale is unclear.
title: T4: Stale PR triage — rebase or close (4 PRs)

description:
Both PRs target the deeper custom audience React work that got descoped from launch. Behind FF tdf-dc-react-custom-audience-rules. Launch shipped with simpler 'echo ruleset' (#123355, merged) instead.

- embercom#123409 (4d) — Audience builder patches: type map coverage, unrepresentable-predicates fallback, mode-flip reset, baseline re-seed. **CONFLICTING + Buildkite FAILURE on react-teammate-app + ui-monorepo.** Stacked on Vedran's now-merged audience work.
- embercom#121984 (13d) — Predicate rules editor for custom audience. Same flag space.

Decision needed: Now that launch shipped with the echo-ruleset approach, do these get revived (rebased + CI fixed) or closed-as-superseded? Talk to Vedran/Zille about whether the deeper React custom-audience UI is still planned for next milestone.

Action: 5-min sync with Vedran or async Slack. Then either rebase+fix or close both.
title: T5: Custom audience deeper PRs decision (#123409 + #121984)

description:
Ellice is releasing ownership of in-progress work (Slack thread C0AS4KQFX16 / 1777045811.018039 on 2026-04-24). 4 items to triage. Decision: which (if any) to adopt vs. let team pick up.

1. **API audiences (ready for review)** — high adoption value, near merge.
   - Issue intercom#501226 — DC Public API: Audience targeting fields (OPEN)
   - PR intercom#500264 (OPEN) ready
   - PR developer-docs#867, OpenAPI#474

2. **Future DC Configure API Work** — Issue intercom#501213 (OPEN). No PR yet.

3. **Full-access DC transformations rollout** — Issue intercom#501173 (OPEN). FF tic-data-connector-full-access-transformations.

4. **Old audiences FF cleanup (ready for review)** — straightforward cleanup.
   - Issue intercom#440094 — tdf-migrate-actions-to-fin-audience FF stale (OPEN)
   - PR intercom#501005 (OPEN), embercom#123710 (OPEN)
   - Admin FF: tdf-migrate-actions-to-fin-audience

Suggested action: adopt #1 (API audiences) — closing 4 PRs delivers public API value. Adopt #4 (FF cleanup) as background work. Defer #2 and #3 unless capacity allows.
title: T6: Ellice farewell handoff — pick what to adopt (4 items)

description:
Items raised in #data-connectors-wg that need product/design resolution. Not directly closeable by code — track for follow-up.

1. **Data Inputs edit/no-edit** (Vedran ↔ Ana, thread 1776789367.832149) — combined data inputs + URL/body attributes is intentional, but edit semantics still undecided. Ana's last question: 'do we still need to make a decision on the edit/no edit or is it fine?' Unanswered.

2. **'Behavior' in Used By column** (Ana, thread 1776349208.173989) — likely legacy custom bots. Hide from UI? 23 replies in thread.

3. **Health metric improvements** (David, thread 1776441912) — David said P2 issue would be filed: add 'avg response size' metric, filter by procedure (P1) and payload size.

4. **DC unhealthy → improvement signaling** (David, same thread) — open philosophical Q: how do we show a DC is improving even if still 'unhealthy'?

5. **Page structure / MCP vs API DC positioning** (David, message 1776871007 with loom) — not GA blocker, but needs broader thinking.

6. **MCP DCs auto-update** (Johnathan, thread 1776960339) — on Q2 roadmap per Steve. External-team interest. Spike candidate.

Action: review monthly; convert to issues/PRs as scope clarifies.
title: T7: Track open design/decision items from Slack
